title: Today's Markets Brief: Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market
summary: A look at the latest market trends and economic updates
imageAlt: Image from source article for Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market
imageCaption: Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market
imageSourceTitle: Why double-digit earnings growth won’t stop the next bear market

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through the latest market updates. Let's dive into what's making headlines today. I've been digging into a couple of stories that caught my eye, and I think they're worth sharing.

First up, a story from MarketWatch that's got everyone talking: despite double-digit earnings growth, the next bear market might be just around the corner. According to history, when S&P 500 profits spike like this, it often signals the final innings of a bull market. My read is that investors are getting a bit nervous, and it's worth keeping an eye on. I mean, who doesn't love a good earnings report, but it's not all sunshine and rainbows.

Next, a concerning update from the New York Fed: low-income households are facing a 'remarkable' rise in food insecurity. This is partly why Americans feel worse off than ever before, even with a strong economy. It just goes to show that not everyone's feeling the benefits of growth. The K-shaped economy is a real thing, folks, and it's leaving some people behind.

And then there's the bigger picture. It's a bit like that old saying, 'a rising tide doesn't lift all boats.' We've got a situation where some people are thriving, but others are struggling to make ends meet. It's a complex issue, but it's something to keep in mind when we're looking at the market trends.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
